sites.name: Mews Lane Assemblage
sites.summary: Inner-suburban townhouse site with existing Archicad concept model and live construction planning data.

sites.name: River Road Infill
sites.summary: Smaller infill site used to compare duplex and compact townhouse feasibility before model authoring.

site_constraints.title: Neighbourhood height control
site_constraints.description: Two-storey street wall is generally acceptable; third-storey bulk requires setback testing.

site_constraints.title: Rear drainage easement
site_constraints.description: Stormwater spine constrains basement and private open-space layouts near the rear boundary.

site_constraints.title: Vehicle access width
site_constraints.description: Crossover and driveway width limit the practical yield for rear dwellings.

site_constraints.title: Flood overlay check
site_constraints.description: Confirm finished floor level and drainage detention requirements before acquisition commitment.

site_templates.name: Inner-suburban townhouse acquisition
site_templates.description: Reusable site branch template for Melbourne townhouse opportunities with planning, finance, and participant defaults.
site_templates.acquisition_strategy: Conditional contract with due diligence, finance, and planning pre-application gates

scenario_templates.name: 4 TH - Sell 3 / Retain 1
scenario_templates.description: Four townhouse branch with three sale settlements and one retained townhouse for long-term equity exposure.

scenario_templates.name: 3 TH - Sell All
scenario_templates.description: Lower-yield branch with larger dwellings and lower planning exposure.

feasibility_templates.name: Optimised retain-one case
feasibility_templates.description: Margin optimiser mode using design, revenue, cost, timing, finance, and retain-one assumptions.

feasibility_branches.name: 4 TH optimised retain-one branch
feasibility_branches.summary: Applies preview-derived margin, funding, retention, and validation assumptions to the 4 townhouse option.

assumption_templates.name: 2.7m ceilings uplift
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Architect and sales agent to confirm premium is credible for this locality.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview assumption: +$40k sale value per townhouse for 2.7m ceilings; +$8k/TH cost is tracked separately.

assumption_templates.name: Terraced row shared-wall saving
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Builder to confirm shared wall, repeated floor plate, and roofline saving.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview assumption: terraced row shares party walls and saves 12-18% on affected build components.

assumption_templates.name: Spring settlement timing uplift
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Sales agent to validate timing uplift against current local comparables.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview assumption: selling in Sep-Nov recovers current vendor discounting.

assumption_templates.name: Senior debt LVR
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Broker or lender to confirm appetite, presale requirements, and covenants.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview range: 60-75% of total development cost, default 70%.

assumption_templates.name: GST margin scheme
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Accountant/solicitor to validate margin scheme wording and contract price structure.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview formula: 1/11 of revenue less land cost base for sold units.

assumption_templates.name: Engineered timber floors + stone benchtops
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Sales agent and builder to confirm finish premium versus upgrade cost for this locality.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: engineered timber over carpet plus 40mm stone over laminate adds about $15k/TH build cost and $50-60k/TH sale uplift; net margin gain per sold unit about $40k.

assumption_templates.name: Double garage instead of single
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Sales agent to validate family buyer premium and architect to confirm footprint feasibility.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: +$25k/TH build cost and +$70k/TH sale uplift; widens buyer pool from couples to families, net gain about $110k across 3 sold and 4 built.

assumption_templates.name: 3-bed + study market as 4-bed
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Sales agent and architect to validate whether the study can credibly market as a fourth bedroom.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: +$8k/TH build cost and +$65k/TH sale uplift; families paying $1.35M+ usually require four bedrooms, net gain about $163k.

assumption_templates.name: Sell 2 units off-market
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Sales agent or buyer network owner to confirm buyer access and flat-fee/off-market path.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: no 2% agent commission on 2 units through network, buyer agents at flat fee, or direct database buyers; saves about $25-28k per unit.

assumption_templates.name: Owner-supply fixtures & appliances
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Builder to confirm contract permits owner-supplied fixtures and warranty/interface exclusions are understood.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: remove builder markup of 20-40% on tapware, appliances, and lighting across 4 units; about $13k saving per unit, $52k total.

assumption_templates.name: Reduce contingency to 6%
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Builder and feasibility lead to confirm fixed-price contract, geotech report, and detailed scope before reducing contingency.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: reduce contingency from 10% to 6% after fixed-price contract, geotech, and detailed scope; frees about $80k on $2M construction.

assumption_templates.name: Shorten timeline by 3 months
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Feasibility lead to confirm pre-application meeting, parallel tendering, and early sales appointment are realistic.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview lever: pre-application council meeting, parallel tender during permit, and appoint selling agent at lodgement; 3 months less interest on about $2.5M average balance saves $55-75k.

assumption_templates.name: Staged build completion
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Builder and lender to confirm staged completion, partial settlements, and debt retirement mechanics.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: staged build completion reduces senior debt interest in all tabs; current scenario impact about $42,782.

assumption_templates.name: SMSF bridging
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: SMSF specialist, lender, and accountant to confirm bridging is permitted and interest cost is modelled.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: adds $200,000 bridge funding during pre-permit and adds bridge interest to project cost.

assumption_templates.name: Immediate retained TH refinance
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Broker to confirm post-completion refinance rate, valuation basis, and timing for retained townhouse debt.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: shows post-completion refinance/holding benefit only; current scenario impact about $24,500.

assumption_templates.name: Early off-plan marketing
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Sales agent to confirm lender-acceptable presale deposits can be secured before construction finance activates.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: adds presale deposits as capital and improves timing/liquidity only; current scenario impact about $306,250.

assumption_templates.name: Builder deferred margin
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Builder to confirm deferred margin terms, risk premium, and payment trigger; this is timing only, not a saving.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: defers payment and reduces construction finance need; current scenario impact about $170,380.

assumption_templates.name: GST input tax credits
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Accountant to confirm GST registration timing, GST-inclusive cost assumptions, and BAS credit timing.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: working capital only unless costs are GST-inclusive; current scenario impact about $221,494.

assumption_templates.name: 5% builder retention hold
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Builder and solicitor to confirm 5% retention holdback wording and defect-release mechanics.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: payment holdback, not a saving; current scenario impact about $121,700.

assumption_templates.name: Conditional contract
assumption_templates.evidence_requirement: Solicitor and feasibility lead to confirm due diligence, finance, and planning conditions remain live until risk is resolved.
assumption_templates.notes: Preview gap lever: reduces capital at risk by about $180,000; does not create cash, funding, or profit.

assumption_applications.notes: Applied to the 4 townhouse option, pending local sales evidence.
Default debt assumption until lender feedback is captured.
Applied only if the design keeps party wall and roofline repetition.
Needs accountant and solicitor sign-off before acquisition commitment.
Applied if finish upgrades are specified and supported by comparable sales.
Applied if the plan can accommodate double garages without undermining yield.
Applied if the study can credibly market as a fourth bedroom.
Applied to test off-market sale of 2 units while retaining agent coverage for remaining stock.
Applied if tender and contract allow owner-supplied fixtures and appliances.
Applied only after fixed-price contract, geotech, and detailed scope reduce contingency risk.
Applied if council, tender, and sales workstreams can be overlapped safely.
Applied to model staged completion and early senior debt retirement during construction.
Applied to cover pre-permit holding with SMSF bridge funding, subject to specialist advice.
Applied to model immediate refinance of retained townhouse after completion.
Applied to model early off-plan deposits as timing/liquidity support.
Applied to model builder deferred margin as payment timing, not profit saving.
Applied to model GST input tax credit timing through construction.
Applied to model 5% builder retention as payment holdback at settlement.
Applied to model conditional contract risk control; it reduces exposure but does not create funding.

assumption_validations.notes: Confirm spring timing uplift and off-market buyer appetite.
Confirm LVR, presale requirements, and staged drawdown assumptions.
Validate shared-wall saving against builder pricing.
Route to accountant and solicitor before contract exchange.
Confirm finish uplift against current townhouse comparable sales.
Validate family buyer premium for double garage product.
Confirm double garage footprint can fit without harming planning or yield.
Confirm study can be marketed as a fourth bedroom for target buyers.
Confirm off-market buyer channel and expected commission saving.
Confirm tender exclusions, warranty boundaries, and owner-supply logistics.
Confirm fixed-price contract, geotech, and detailed scope support lower contingency.
Confirm programme compression plan and finance saving assumptions.
Confirm staged completion and early settlement handover are buildable.
Confirm lender allows staged settlements to reduce senior debt interest.
Confirm SMSF bridge funding, interest treatment, and compliance requirements.
Confirm retained townhouse refinance LVR, rate, valuation, and timing.
Confirm early off-plan marketing can produce lender-acceptable deposits.
Confirm deferred margin amount, payment trigger, and risk premium.
Confirm GST registration and input tax credit timing before costs are incurred.
Confirm builder accepts 5% retention hold and release mechanics.
Confirm conditional contract clauses reduce exposure and remain enforceable.

assumption_evidence.title: Spring sale comparables required
assumption_evidence.notes: Attach comparable sales or agent memo before raising confidence.

assumption_evidence.title: GST margin scheme advice required
assumption_evidence.notes: Accountant/solicitor advice should confirm cost-base and sale-contract wording.

assumption_evidence.title: Double garage comparable sales required
assumption_evidence.notes: Attach sales evidence showing family buyer premium for double garage townhouse product.

assumption_evidence.title: Off-market buyer channel evidence required
assumption_evidence.notes: Confirm buyer database, flat-fee agent pathway, or buyer-agent access before relying on commission saving.

assumption_evidence.title: Fixed-price contract and geotech evidence required
assumption_evidence.notes: Attach fixed-price contract, geotech report, and detailed scope before reducing contingency from 10% to 6%.

assumption_evidence.title: Compressed programme evidence required
assumption_evidence.notes: Document pre-application meeting, parallel tender, and early sales appointment plan.

assumption_evidence.title: Staged completion and settlement plan required
assumption_evidence.notes: Document staged completion dates, settlement sequence, and debt retirement assumptions.

assumption_evidence.title: SMSF bridging advice required
assumption_evidence.notes: Attach SMSF specialist advice, lending terms, and interest cost treatment.

assumption_evidence.title: Early off-plan deposit strategy required
assumption_evidence.notes: Show how deposits are obtained before construction finance activates and whether lender accepts them.

assumption_evidence.title: Builder deferred margin term sheet required
assumption_evidence.notes: Attach builder agreement showing deferred amount, trigger, and any price premium.

assumption_evidence.title: GST registration and BAS timing evidence required
assumption_evidence.notes: Confirm GST registration before costs, GST-inclusive invoices, and expected credit timing.

assumption_evidence.title: Conditional contract clauses required
assumption_evidence.notes: Attach due diligence, finance, and planning pre-application conditions before treating risk as reduced.

assumption_actions.title: Validate spring sale timing uplift
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Revenue uplift may be overstated, causing the optimised branch to overstate margin.
assumption_actions.notes: Ask sales agent for current comparable evidence and buyer demand notes.

assumption_actions.title: Confirm lender LVR and presale conditions
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Funding gap may remain hidden until the contract is unconditional.
assumption_actions.notes: Broker to validate senior debt appetite and whether presales are required.

assumption_actions.title: Validate double garage premium
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Revenue uplift may be overstated if buyer premium is not supported by comparable sales.
assumption_actions.notes: Ask sales agent for family-buyer comparable evidence and price delta.

assumption_actions.title: Confirm off-market buyer pathway
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Sales cost saving may disappear if buyer channel is not ready before launch.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm buyer database, agent flat-fee option, and which units can be sold off-market.

assumption_actions.title: Approve lower contingency gate
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Project margin may rely on risk allowance reduction before construction risk is genuinely transferred.
assumption_actions.notes: Only approve 6% contingency after fixed-price contract, geotech, and detailed scope are complete.

assumption_actions.title: Validate 3-month programme compression
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Finance savings may be overstated if approvals, tender, or sales milestones cannot overlap.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm council pre-app path, parallel tender schedule, and sales appointment at lodgement.

assumption_actions.title: Validate staged completion debt reduction
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Interest saving may be overstated if completion and settlements cannot be staged.
assumption_actions.notes: Coordinate builder programme, lender conditions, and settlement sequence.

assumption_actions.title: Obtain SMSF bridging advice
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Bridge funding may be unavailable or non-compliant when pre-permit holding costs arrive.
assumption_actions.notes: Get specialist advice before relying on SMSF bridge funding.

assumption_actions.title: Confirm retained townhouse refinance terms
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Post-completion finance saving may not be available at the assumed rate or valuation.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm refinance product, valuation basis, LVR, and timing.

assumption_actions.title: Validate early off-plan deposit path
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Working capital may be overstated if deposits are not secured or lender-accepted.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm campaign timing, deposit amounts, and lender acceptance.

assumption_actions.title: Negotiate builder deferred margin
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Deferred payment benefit may be unavailable or offset by a price premium.
assumption_actions.notes: Document deferred margin amount, trigger, and pricing trade-off.

assumption_actions.title: Confirm GST input credit timing
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Late GST registration can remove input credits from working capital assumptions.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm GST registration before construction costs and BAS timing.

assumption_actions.title: Agree 5% builder retention hold
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Retention holdback may not be available to cover settlement timing gap.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm retention amount and defect release terms in the building contract.

assumption_actions.title: Confirm conditional contract gates
assumption_actions.risk_if_delayed: Going unconditional removes the primary option-risk control before feasibility risks are closed.
assumption_actions.notes: Confirm due diligence, finance, and planning conditions with solicitor.

simulation_templates.name: Margin and funding risk Monte Carlo
simulation_templates.description: Future simulation preset that samples land, sale, build, duration, finance, contingency, and timing assumptions.

scenario_options.name: 3 Townhouse Premium
scenario_options.summary: Lower planning risk with larger dwellings, stronger end value, and current baseline schedule.
scenario_options.pinned_reason: Most balanced option: strong planning fit, current Archicad link, and margin above target.

scenario_options.name: 4 Townhouse Yield
scenario_options.summary: Higher yield option using the recovery scenario for tighter sequencing and sales release testing.
scenario_options.pinned_reason: Worth watching for yield upside if planning and programme risk can be contained.

scenario_options.name: Duplex Base Case
scenario_options.summary: Early feasibility option without an Archicad model; used to test acquisition and planning constraints.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: Low
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Assumes no authority-driven redesign and stable civil scope.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: Mid
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Base estimating case aligned to current package and schedule model.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: High
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Includes services easement risk and extended approval period.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: Low
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Assumes compact layouts pass with minor design changes.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: Mid
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Includes additional facade and fire-separation allowances.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: High
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Planning redesign and slower sales release sensitivity.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: Low
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Clean planning pathway and no flood mitigation allowance.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: Mid
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Screening case pending drainage and access advice.

scenario_cost_ranges.label: High
scenario_cost_ranges.notes: Includes flood overlay and slower authority pathway sensitivity.

sales_assumptions.notes: Premium larger townhouse product with staggered private treaty release.
Higher count with slightly lower average price and longer release.
Comparable infill duplex sales from recent nearby campaigns.

archicad_links.model_scope: baseline zones, siteworks, facade screen, and package-linked elements
draft yield model linked to recovery schedule scenario

site_resources.title: Planning Property Report
site_resources.notes: Source for council, zoning, overlays, site area, and constraints summary.

site_resources.title: Comparable Listing Research
site_resources.notes: Comparable townhouse and townhouse-site sale research for revenue assumptions.

site_resources.notes: Authority source for planning controls, application pathway, and lodgement guidance.

site_planning_highlights.heritage_status: No individual heritage overlay identified in source report
site_planning_highlights.flood_status: No flood overlay identified; verify drainage authority comments
site_planning_highlights.vegetation_status: Street tree and rear vegetation retention to be confirmed
site_planning_highlights.easements: Rear drainage easement noted along south boundary; maintain authority access during design.
site_planning_highlights.planning_summary: Townhouse yield appears plausible subject to height, overlooking, neighbourhood character, and drainage easement constraints.

network_organisations.name: Mews Lane Development Co.
network_organisations.description: Principal developer and feasibility sponsor for site acquisition, strategy, delivery, and exit decisions.

network_organisations.name: Authorities and Certifiers
network_organisations.description: Planning, utilities, building surveyor, and statutory reviewers represented as advisory profiles.

network_organisations.name: Design and Specialist Consultants
network_organisations.description: Architectural, planning, civil, engineering, market, tax, legal, and finance advice network.

network_organisations.name: Delivery Supply Chain
network_organisations.description: Builder, subcontractor, fabricator, supplier, and waste management inputs for buildability and pricing.

network_organisations.name: Agentic Advisory Bench
network_organisations.description: LLM-ready specialist agents governed by project scope, evidence, tool policies, and human review.

network_profiles.summary: Frames the investment thesis, target margin, risk appetite, hold/sell strategy, and go/no-go decisions.
network_profiles.contact_details:
principal@mewslane.example
Preferred channel: weekly feasibility review

network_profiles.domain: Cost, revenue, program, and scenario modelling
network_profiles.summary: Owns the feasibility model, scenario assumptions, sensitivity checks, and decision papers.
Surfaces likely planning objections, approval pathway risks, information requests, and conditions.

network_profiles.domain: Yield, massing, amenity, Archicad model intent
network_profiles.summary: Coordinates design scenarios, model assumptions, envelope constraints, and product quality.
Challenges construction methodology, trade sequencing, cost risk, lead times, and site logistics.

network_profiles.domain: Product mix, buyer demand, pricing, campaign strategy
network_profiles.summary: Tests product-market fit, buyer objections, pricing bands, presale strategy, and absorption rate.
Assesses debt appetite, contingency, interest cover, presale hurdles, and credit committee concerns.

network_profiles.domain: Buyer needs, objections, willingness to pay
network_profiles.summary: Represents purchaser motivations around parking, storage, outdoor space, schools, and price sensitivity.
Represents adjacent resident concerns that can influence planning response and construction logistics.
LLM-ready agent that reviews site constraints, planning notes, scenarios, and authority questions.

network_profiles.domain: Master costs, packages, source evidence, scenario ranges
network_profiles.summary: LLM-ready agent that compares master database rates, template links, and scenario cost plan deltas.

network_profiles.domain: Multi-agent inquiry routing and review workflow
network_profiles.summary: Coordinates participants, asks follow-up questions, and converts inquiry outputs into reviewed work products.

network_profile_capabilities.base_knowledge: Uses current sites, scenarios, cost ranges, sales assumptions, and schedule data.
network_profile_capabilities.scope: Can propose assumptions and work products; project mutations require human approval.

network_profile_capabilities.base_knowledge: Reads master cost database items, code catalogue links, template items, scenario cost plans, and source notes.
network_profile_capabilities.scope: Advisory only until a human accepts a cost database or scenario update.
constraints_json: Do not silently update scenario snapshots

network_profile_capabilities.base_knowledge: Reads site constraints, scenario summaries, Archicad links, and planning fit notes.
network_profile_capabilities.scope: Can prepare questions and review notes; cannot represent formal planning advice.
constraints_json: Avoid legal conclusions without planner review

network_knowledge_packs.title: Feasibility Control Pack
network_knowledge_packs.instructions: Compare scenarios using site constraints, cost ranges, sales assumptions, and schedule impacts. Separate facts, assumptions, and decisions.
constraints_json: No automatic changes to approved scenario snapshots
network_knowledge_packs.output_policy: Decision-ready outputs require human review before becoming project records.

network_knowledge_packs.title: Planning and Authority Pack
network_knowledge_packs.instructions: Interrogate constraints, likely authority questions, overlays, and neighbour concerns before committing to design yield.
constraints_json: Flag when formal consultant advice is needed
network_knowledge_packs.output_policy: Authority-facing material must be reviewed by the nominated human planner.

network_knowledge_packs.title: Cost Intelligence Pack
network_knowledge_packs.domain: Master cost database, package pricing, source confidence
network_knowledge_packs.instructions: Use linked master costs and source notes to explain pricing confidence, gaps, and package-level risk.
network_knowledge_packs.output_policy: Cost outputs are advisory until accepted into the master database or a scenario revision.

network_inquiries.title: Can the 4 townhouse option survive planning and still hit margin?
network_inquiries.question: Compare the 3 townhouse premium and 4 townhouse yield options across planning risk, cost confidence, saleability, and program pressure.

network_inquiries.title: Which master cost rates need market validation before acquisition?
network_inquiries.question: Identify rates in the townhouse template with weak source confidence or high package sensitivity.

network_inquiry_messages.message: The 4 townhouse option should be tested against rear setback, overlooking, parking, and third-storey bulk before relying on the higher revenue case.
Four smaller dwellings may broaden the buyer pool, but the loss of private open space and parking certainty needs a buyer objection check.
Facade, drainage, and preliminaries should be repriced first because they touch both package risk and planning-response scope.

network_work_products.title: Planning risk brief for 4 townhouse yield case
network_work_products.summary: Draft brief listing likely RFI items, design mitigations, and authority-facing questions before the next concept model revision.

network_work_products.title: Cost source gap report
network_work_products.summary: Identifies master database items requiring live subcontractor checks before acquisition pricing is locked.

network_work_product_links.notes: Use with the yield scenario planning review.

network_agent_cards.system_instructions: Act as an advisory planning-risk analyst. Use project evidence and mark uncertainty clearly.
network_agent_cards.context_policy: May read site, scenario, constraint, Archicad-link, and inquiry records. Writes require human review.

network_agent_cards.system_instructions: Act as an advisory cost-intelligence analyst. Never mutate scenario snapshots without approval.
network_agent_cards.context_policy: May read master cost, code catalogue, source, template, and scenario cost plan records.

network_agent_sessions.title: Yield risk roundtable
network_agent_sessions.objective: Coordinate planning, cost, sales, and developer perspectives before choosing a preferred scenario.

network_agent_messages.content: Frame the comparison as approval probability, revenue upside, cost confidence, and decision reversibility.

network_agent_tool_calls.output_summary: Returned cost ranges, GFA, dwellings, sales assumptions, and linked template data.

network_agent_outputs.title: Initial yield-risk synthesis
network_agent_outputs.summary: Proceed with the 4 townhouse option only after planning massing tests and targeted subcontractor repricing.
